phase: Phase 7 Display / Graphics Minimal Full Vertical
scope: Add FramebufferBenchmarkRecord as first-class semantic display benchmark evidence
Add RecordFramebufferBenchmark command and FramebufferBenchmarkRecorded event
Require exact Store, DisplayCapability, FramebufferWrite, FramebufferFlushRegion, DisplayEventLog, DisplaySnapshotBarrier, DisplayObject, and FramebufferObject generations
Derive throughput and flush-rate metrics from sample bytes, sample frames, and measured nanos
Require a quiescent display snapshot barrier before benchmark evidence is accepted
Expose framebuffer-benchmark stable ViewV1 and graph historical edges through osctl
no_goals: real framebuffer present or scanout timing

findings_fixed: Framebuffer benchmark metrics use exact object refs and generations instead of display labels
Benchmark validation rejects stale snapshot barriers and non-closed timing metrics
osctl ViewV1 reports semantic benchmark metrics without leaking adapter internals
Benchmark history does not create live framebuffer ownership
bad_smells_checked: No real scanout timing was claimed
No GPU or third-party graphics dependency was introduced
No raw framebuffer bytes became benchmark truth
osctl emits ViewV1 JSON rather than internal Rust structs
benchmark evidence is explicitly tied to G10 quiescent snapshot barrier evidence
residual_notes: Phase 8 owns integrated display panic and scheduler-load scenarios
Phase 9 owns final framebuffer flush latency experiment matrix
Future real display backend must keep scanout timing as adapter evidence, not semantic truth

residual_gaps: Phase 8 owns integrated display panic and scheduler-load scenarios
Phase 9 owns final framebuffer flush latency experiment matrix
Future real display backend must keep scanout timing as adapter evidence, not semantic truth
